items.title: E-bike rider dies after colliding with public bus in Perth's north-east

children.content: A 50-year-old man dies at the scene after his e-bike is involved in a crash with a Transperth bus at an intersection in Swan View, with police appealing for witnesses.

gallery.altText: Blue and white tape across a road

thumbnail.altText: Blue and white tape across a road

items.title: 'Large crocodile' attacks man in WA's far north

children.content: A man in his 20s has been flown to a Perth hospital after he was attacked by a crocodile at a popular camping spot north of Broome.

items.title: Former footballer Barry Cable found not guilty of sexually abusing child

children.content: Former Australian Rules footballer Barry Cable has been found not guilty of sexually abusing a child in Perth in the 1960s.

image.altText: Barry Cable walks up the stairs to court followed by a media pack.

items.title: Residents urged to stay in as burnt battery product may react with forecast rain

children.content: Emergency services say a fire at the Li-ion Energy site in Maddington in Perth's south-east  — the biggest blaze of its kind in WA's history — will be reviewed to inform future responses to lithium battery fires.

articles.title: Schools deemed safe after WA's biggest lithium-ion battery fire

children.content: Schools in Perth's south-east have been deemed safe for students to attend after WA's biggest lithium-ion battery fire sparked a HAZMAT warning in Maddington, which is still in place.

image.altText: Flames can be seen billowing from a building in an industrial area.

Flames can be seen billowing from a building in an industrial area.

thumbnail.altText: Flames can be seen billowing from a building in an industrial area.
Flames can be seen billowing from a building in an industrial area.

items.title: Father calls for accountability over 21-month-old son's death

children.content: The father of Sandipan Dhar, who died after not receiving a blood test at Joondalup Health Campus, says there is still a lack of accountability over his son's death.

image.altText: A boy dressed in winter clothes sitting on a black couch

items.title: Tow truck driver weeps after sentencing for fatal crash

children.content: Ross David Gamage has been fined $5,000 and had his licence suspended for six months over a 2023 crash that killed a 45-year-old man near Waroona.

image.altText: Rpss Gamage walks out of the Bunbury Courthouse after the decision.

items.title: Prospector couldn't believe his luck when he found rare australite

children.content: The object formed when a meteorite slammed into Earth more than 800,000 years ago.

gallery.altText: a small, round, black, slightly pitted rock held between man's fingers
Older man in red shirt holds black tektite while smiling

thumbnail.altText: a small, round, black, slightly pitted rock held between man's fingers
Older man in red shirt holds black tektite while smiling

items.title: Australia's biggest AI factory planned for remote WA

children.content: A huge artificial intelligence data centre capable of training models like ChatGPT is set to be built south of Broome in WA's Kimberley at a cost of billons of dollars.

items.title: PM to convene national cabinet to tackle 'long tail' fuel crisis

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has revealed he will be convening national cabinet again in the coming days. Follow live.

items.title: Did you really get a discount on your Tim Tams? The ACCC doesn't think so

children.content: The sequel to Australia's supermarket pricing wars is about to kick off in the federal court as the ACCC takes on Woolworths over what it argues are misleading pricing practices.

image.altText: Close up of the Woolworths logo and a special sign in the background.

items.title: 'Pretty invisible': The rise of ADHD medication being prescribed to young women

children.content: Within the sharp increase of ADHD rates in Australia lies a hidden story — more women are being prescribed medication than men. Understanding why is more complicated.

image.altText: A graphic image with silhouettes of men and women's side profiles overlaid with medication and two upwards arrows.

items.title: Gas tax fight escalates ahead of budget as inquiry begins

children.content: Calls are growing for a new 25 per cent tax on gas exports to increase the petroleum resource rent tax on windfall profits, but companies have warned any changes would jeopardise Australia's energy supply.

image.altText: David Pocock at a press conference at Parliament House

items.title: Push to tax gas companies 'ripping up our oceans' as Gorgon plan approved

children.content: Natural gas taxes are back in the fray after the expansion of Chevron's Gorgon project off WA's north-west coast received environmental approval amid a Senate inquiry into how resources are taxed.

gallery.altText: A drone image of Chevron's Gorgon project on Barrow Island.
Gas plant infrastructure on a scrubby island at sunrise.
A shot of tall buildings in Perth, with a focus on the Chevron building, with some leaves hanging in front.

thumbnail.altText: A drone image of Chevron's Gorgon project on Barrow Island.
Gas plant infrastructure on a scrubby island at sunrise.
A shot of tall buildings in Perth, with a focus on the Chevron building, with some leaves hanging in front.

items.title: For the first time in decades, cash use in Australia has increased

children.content: A new report has found cash use has dropped by more than 50 per cent in almost 20 years. But there has been a slight increase in cash payments since 2022.

items.title: Where does all the NDIS money go and how did we get here?

children.content: With more changes surrounding the NDIS set to be announced, here’s everything you need to know about the scheme.

image.altText: A graphic showing a child running, an NDIS logo, a calculator and arrows on a green background

items.title: Six ways your smartwatch is lying to you, according to science

children.content: Wearable fitness technologies like smartwatches are worn by millions of people daily. But they're not as accurate as you might think.

image.altText: A woman in a red T-shirt looks down at a watch next to swimming pool

items.title: Red-hot Perth ace Green blazes to third LA Championship triumph

children.content: Australian golf ace Hannah Green wins her fourth title of the season, taking out the LPGA Tour's LA Championship for a third time in four years.

image.altText: An Australian golfer smiles as she stands on a green holding a large trophy after winning an LPGA tournament.

items.title: This country music singer's tour costs have got her rethinking her career

children.content: Adele Oliver admits being a country music artist has "never been a great career choice", but she says things are worse than ever, with her last gig costing her money due to the escalating price of fuel.

items.title: Living in an apartment with your dog? Here are four things to consider

children.content: Providing opportunities for your dog to explore, giving them a safe and comfortable space to retreat to — as well as a good routine — are key to keeping your dog happy.

image.altText: small brown dog laying in blue furry bed

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?

children.content: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

image.altText: A man looks surprised and another man looks excited in a classroom

items.title: ASX set to open in the green, oil keeps rising

children.content: The ASX 200 is set to open higher in morning trading, after losses on Wall Street. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Automation is growing in Australia's mining industry — but what are the costs?

children.content: At Australia's largest gold mine, drivers are more likely to be found in a control room than a cabin, but researchers and unions say jobs and safety remain significant concerns.

items.title: Couple says loophole allowing theft from cars shipped interstate

children.content: Elihu and Amanda Del Valle say thousands of dollars worth of their belongings were stolen from the boot of their car when it was shipped from Melbourne to Perth, but police aren't able to investigate.

items.title: Four metres of WWII 'short snorter' banknotes signify mateship

children.content: It is a crime to deface banknotes, but these rare wartime notes covered in scribbles cause excitement whenever they appear.

image.altText: man in checkered shirt sitting at table with rows of coloured banknotes

items.title: Giant's debut and a taunted Aussie in spotlight as NBA playoffs begin

children.content: The first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs is officially underway. Here are the key things to look out for in each series.

image.altText: Victor Wembanyama celebrates by holding two fingers up

items.title: Fuel prices no obstacle as passionate photographers hit the road to chase auroras

children.content: For Leonie and Dean McFaull, fuel prices aren't stopping them crossing the country to add to their capture of more than 100 auroras.
